title: CompactSize Encoding
description: CompactSize is Bitcoin's variable-length integer encoding for counts and byte lengths in serialized protocol data. Understanding its prefixes is necessary for parsing transaction, block, script, and network messages correctly.

resources.title: Bitcoin Wiki: Protocol Documentation
resources.notes: Wire-protocol table for CompactSize prefixes, encoded widths, and integer ranges.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core Developer Documentation: Serialization
resources.notes: Reference context for CompactSize fields and canonical size encodings in transaction serialization.

resources.title: Programming Bitcoin
resources.notes: Hands-on treatment of parsing and serializing Bitcoin transaction data.
